mnemonics.title: SELect Events Like a Pro
mnemonics.content: SEL: Source → Event → Listener. Remember event handling sequence: 1) Event Source generates Event 2) Creates Event object 3) Listener handles it

mnemonics.title: My Very Cool App
mnemonics.content: MVC: Model (Data) ↔ View (UI) ↔ Controller (Logic). Imagine three team members passing work in order: M prepares data → V displays → C mediates

mnemonics.title: RIH Event Handling
mnemonics.content: RIH: Register Listeners → Implement Methods → Handle Events. Three essential steps for any Swing interaction

mnemonics.title: PLAF is a Palette
mnemonics.content: Pluggable Look & Feel (PLAF) = Like choosing color themes. Visualize swapping UI skins like changing desktop wallpapers

acronyms.usage: Core architecture pattern for separating data, presentation, and control logic
Remember event handling components: Source → Event → Listener
Key Swing feature allowing UI theme customization
Critical for thread-safe GUI updates in Swing

visualTips.visualization: Imagine three transparent overlays: Blue layer (Model - database), Green layer (View - buttons/forms), Red layer (Controller - wires connecting them)
Picture a relay race: User action (batton) → Event object (runner) → Listener (finish line)
Visualize a theme switcher panel with Windows/Metal/Nimbus options as clothing styles for your UI
Imagine a single-file turnstile - all UI updates must queue here to prevent collisions

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant: Model (kitchen/store), View (dining area), Controller (waiters taking orders)
Customer service desk: You (event source) generate ticket (event) → Specialist (listener) resolves it
Smartphone themes - same apps but different visual styles (iOS vs Material Design)
Conveyor belt in factory - all UI operations must be placed on it to avoid assembly line chaos
